channel.title: Fertility and Sperm on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Fertility and Sperm on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Higher vitamin D results in 22% fewer abnormal sperm (Mendelian analysis)
item.description:
• Causal association between 25-Hydroxyvitamin D and risk of abnormal spermatozoa: a bidirectional Mendelian randomization study

Causal association between 25-Hydroxyvitamin D and risk of abnormal spermatozoa: a bidirectional Mendelian randomization study

Reprod Toxicol. 2024 Apr 19:108597. doi: 10.1016/j.reprotox.2024.108597 PDF behind paywall

Nannan Li 1, Ke Yang 1, Youjie Zeng 2, Si Cao 3, Liang Deng 4

Previous studies indicated conflicting findings regarding the association between vitamin D and abnormal spermatozoa. Herein, we assessed the causal association between circulating 25-Hydroxyvitamin D (25OHD) levels and the risk of abnormal spermatozoa by utilizing bidirectional Mendelian randomization (MR) analysis. Genome-wide association study summary statistics for 25OHD and abnormal spermatozoa were obtained from publicly accessible databases. Single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) associated with 25OHD and SNPs associated with abnormal spermatozoa were used as instrumental variables (IVs) for forward MR analysis and reverse MR analysis, respectively. Inverse variance weighted (IVW) was the main MR approach, while weighted median, MR-Egger and maximum likelihood methods were employed to supplement IVW. In addition, several sensitivity tests assessed the reliability of MR analysis. Forward MR analysis showed that elevated 25OHD levels significantly reduced abnormal spermatozoa risk (odds ratio [OR] = 0.75, 95% confidence interval [CI]: 0.56-1.00, P = 4.98E-02), and the effect remained statistically significant after excluding SNPs associated with confounders ( OR = 0.73 , 95% CI: 0.54-0.98, P = 3.83E-02) or only utilizing SNPs located near 25OHD-associated genes only as IVs (OR = 0.58, 95% CI: 0.41-0.81, P = 1.67E-03). Reverse MR analysis indicated abnormal spermatozoa not affecting 25OHD level (P > 0.05). Sensitivity tests showed that MR analyses were not affected by heterogeneity and horizontal polytropy.

item.title: In vitro fertilization and Vitamin D – many studies
item.description:
• IVF newborns had ~2 ng/mL higher Vitamin D levels - April 2024
      • Fertility improved the most if give Vitamin D AND 1 or more Vitamin D Receptor Activators

IVF newborns had ~2 ng/mL higher Vitamin D levels - April 2024

Vitamin D3 among neonates born after in vitro fertilization compared with neonates from the general population

Acta Obstet Gynecol Scand. 2024;00:1–10. DOI: 10.1111/aogs.14819

Karen Christina Walker1© | Sofie Gry Pristed2,3 | Fanney Thorsteinsdottir1,4 Ina Olmer Specht1,4© | Arieh Cohen5 | Berit Lilienthal Heitmann 1,3,6© |

item.title: 90% in US have chlormequat chloride in urine – which is linked to reduced fertility
item.description:
Americans Exposed to Fertility-Lowering Chemicals in Cheerios, Quaker Oats

Mercola   PDF

92% of (non-ogranic) oat-based foods, including popular brands like Quaker Oats and Cheerios, also contained the chemical

EPA Considers Expanding Chlormequat (a pesticide) Use in Crops
Date %
2017 69%
2018-2022 74%
2023 90%

VitaminDWiki - Pesticides 8 X less likely to result in female infertility if take vitamin D – Sept 2023

VitaminDWiki – Pesticides increase risk of Cancers, Alz, ALS, Asthma, ADHD, etc. (all related to low vitamin D) – Oct 2016

item.title: More fertility associated with higher vitamin D
item.description:
This quickly posted page has minimal formatting, links, etc.

Fertil Steril . 2023 Dec 23:S0015-0282(23)02094-0. doi: 10.1016/j.fertnstert.2023.12.023. Online ahead of print.

Association between Serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D and Antimüllerian Hormone in a Cohort of African-American Women

Anita Subramanian 1, Quaker E Harmon 2, Lia A Bernardi 3, Mercedes R Carnethon 4, Erica E Marsh 5, Donna D Baird 2, Anne Marie Z Jukic 2

item.title: Infertility not associated with vitamin D (virtually none had more than 30 ng)
item.description:
• Seasonal Serum 25(OH) Vitamin D Level and Reproductive or Immune Markers in Reproductive-Aged Women with Infertility: A Cross-Sectional Observational Study in East Japan

Seasonal Serum 25(OH) Vitamin D Level and Reproductive or Immune Markers in Reproductive-Aged Women with Infertility: A Cross-Sectional Observational Study in East Japan

Nutrients 2023, 15(24), 5059; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu15245059

by Kuniaki Ota 1,2,3,*ORCID,Junichiro Mitsui 3,4,Shoko Katsumata 3,4,Yuko Takayanagi 3,Yurie Nako 3,Makiko Tajima 3,Akira Komiya 3,Toshifumi Takahashi 1ORCID andKiyotaka Kawai 3,4ORCID

item.title: Infertile women have low vitamin D
item.description:
• Relationship between Serum 25-Hydroxyvitamin D Levels and Hormonal Status in Infertile Women: A Retrospective Study

Relationship between Serum 25-Hydroxyvitamin D Levels and Hormonal Status in Infertile Women: A Retrospective Study

Diagnostics (Basel). 2023 Sep 22;13(19):3024. doi: 10.3390/diagnostics13193024.

Melinda Kolcsár 1, Bernadett Berecki 2, Zsolt Gáll 1

Background: Infertility is a prevalent issue that affects 10-18% of couples worldwide, and up to 30% in Eastern Europe. Infertility of unknown etiology is one of the most challenging forms of infertility. Vitamin D has recently been extensively studied and researchers are investigating its possible role in ovulation and pregnancy. While the evidence suggests a positive association between vitamin D and ovulation, more research is needed to understand the role that vitamin D plays in ovulation.

item.title: Infertility risk increased 2.5 X by poor Vitamin D Receptor
item.description:
• Genetic association of vitamin D receptor gene with female infertility
      • VitaminDWiki - The risk of 44+ diseases at least double with poor Vitamin D Receptor
      • VitaminDWiki - Vitamin D Receptor activation can be increased in 14 ways

Genetic association of vitamin D receptor gene with female infertility

Nucleosides Nucleotides Nucleic Acids . 2023 Jul 26;1-18. doi: 10.1080/15257770.2023.2236167   PDF is behind a $58 paywall

Mussarat Ashraf 1, Haq Nawaz Khan 1, Rabab Ibrahim 1, Maheen Shahid 1, Sher Khan 1, Ambrin Fatima 1, Saif Ullah 2, Rehana Rehman 1

item.title: Infertile women had less Vitamin D (Baghdad in this case)
item.description:
• Prevalence of 25-Hydroxyvitamin D (Vitamin D) Deficiency in a Group of Infertile Women from Baghdad City

Prevalence of 25-Hydroxyvitamin D (Vitamin D) Deficiency in a Group of Infertile Women from Baghdad City

Biochemistry Research International Vol 2023 https://doi.org/10.1155/2023/6597730

Muthana Anad Majid ,1 Wafaa Nasser Hassan ,2 a nd Amna Fadhil Ridha 3

Chart of data made by VitaminDWiki

Background. Infertility is a common issue affecting a large number of Iraqi women of reproductive age. The relationship between vitamin D deficiency and infertility has previously drawn the attention of gynecologists, and an increasing number of vitamin D testing has been requested.

item.title: Vitamin D improves Infertility in 9 ways (50 references, PDF behind paywall)
item.description:
• Vitamin D and infertility

Vitamin D and infertility

Curr Opin Obstet Gynecol . 2023 May 22. doi: 10.1097/GCO.0000000000000887

Samantha Simpson 1, Lubna Pal 2

Purpose of review: Vitamin D deficiency has been implicated as a contributing factor to a spectrum of reproductive health burden, including

  • difficulty conceiving,

  • pathogenesis of gynaecological disorders such as

    • uterine fibroids and endometriosis, to

  • metabolic and endocrine burden of polycystic ovarian syndrome (PCOS).

Recent findings: There have been recent publications showing that in infertile women who are supplemented with vitamin D,

item.title: Infertile patients 1.7X more-likely to become pregnant if take Vitamin D – meta-analysis
item.description:
• Influence of Vitamin D supplementation on reproductive outcomes of infertile patients: a systematic review and meta-analysis
      • 1.7X better on-average when taking any amount of vitamin D - more is better
      • Greatly helps if the male takes vitamin D as well
      • If not using a loading dose, will take 2-6 months to benefit from the Vitamin D

Influence of Vitamin D supplementation on reproductive outcomes of infertile patients: a systematic review and meta-analysis

Reproductive Biology and Endocrinology volume 21 https://doi.org/10.1186/s12958-023-01068-8

item.title: Unknown cause (idopathic) for male Infertility appears to be low vitamin D (9 X)

item.title: PCOS also fought by Magnesium combinations – review
item.description:
• The effect of magnesium alone or its combination with other supplements on the markers of inflammation, OS and metabolism in women with polycystic ovarian syndrome (PCOS): A systematic review
      • Suspect Omega-3 could also fight PCOS
      • See also VitaminDWiki

The effect of magnesium alone or its combination with other supplements on the markers of inflammation, OS and metabolism in women with polycystic ovarian syndrome (PCOS): A systematic review

Front Endocrinol (Lausanne). 2022 Aug 5 doi: 10.3389/fendo.2022.974042

item.title: Conception was 3.7X more likely if the male had a good level of Vitamin D

Serum Vitamin D Concentrations, Time to Pregnancy, and Pregnancy Outcomes among Preconception Couples: A Cohort Study in Shanghai, China

Nutrients 2022, 14(15), 3058; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu14153058

by Yu Zhang 1,2ORCID,Anne Marie Z. Jukic 3,Heqing Song 1,Lifeng Zhang 4,Fengyun Yang 4,Shoule Wu 4,Dongxiao Yin 4 and Hong Jiang 1,*

item.title: Better sperm associated with higher vitamin D (again)
item.description:

item.title: Fertility is a function of vitamin D around each egg (follicular fluid) – many studies
item.description:
• Low vitamin D levels in follicular fluid, but not in serum, are associated with adverse outcomes in assisted reproduction - Feb 2022
      • The level of vitamin D in follicular fluid and ovarian reserve in an in vitro fertilization program: A pilot study (all levels <25 ng) - May 2022
      • FF Vit D levels corrlated with pregnancy test, oocyte count - (did not consider VDR) - July 2022
      • Follicular Fluid and follicles
      • Agreed: Local level of Vitamin D is more important than blood/serum level Vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki - Genetics chart (Receptor is the most important and easily changed)
      • The risk of 44 diseases at least double with poor Vitamin D Receptor
      • VitaminDWiki - Vitamin D Receptor activation can be increased in 13+ ways:

Low vitamin D levels in follicular fluid, but not in serum, are associated with adverse outcomes in assisted reproduction - Feb 2022

Archives of Gynecology and Obstetrics V 305, pages 505–517 https://doi.org/10.1007/s00404-021-06174-9

item.title: In vitro fertilization is more successful when vitamin D in ovaries, not just blood, is increased
item.description:

item.title: Conception 1.5 X more likely if taking any amount of Omega-3
item.description:
• Omega-3 fatty acid supplementation and fecundability

Omega-3 fatty acid supplementation and fecundability

Hum Reprod . 2022 Feb 11;deac027. doi: 10.1093/humrep/deac027 Hiding behind a $47 paywall

J Stanhiser 1 2 3, A M Z Jukic 4, D R McConnaughey 5, A Z Steiner 6

Many of the women took Omega-3 daily

Women taking Omega-3 were also taking lots more vitamin D and prenatal vitamins

images taken from DeepDyve.com

Study question: Is self-reported use of omega-3 fatty acid supplements associated with fecundability, the probability of natural conception, in a given menstrual cycle?

item.description:

item.title: Pregnancy loss 9X more likely if male had less than 20 ng of Vitamin D
item.description:
Trends in Self-reported Spontaneous Abortions: 1970–2000 - Aug 2012

  • “After accounting for abortion availability and the characteristics of pregnant women, the rate of reported miscarriages increased by about 1.0% per year. "

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Speculation by VitaminDWiki: Might the increased miscarriage rate be associated with decreased Vitamin D levels?

Why all companies should offer leave for pregnancy loss Fast Company July 2021

  • “A 2020 study found that one in six women who have a miscarriage or an ectopic pregnancy suffer from long-term post-traumatic stress. When you consider that anywhere from 10-20% of known pregnancies end in miscarriage , that’s a lot of people who likely end up suffering privately at work, unable to take time off to recuperate.”

Objective: To determine the association between vitamin D levels in the male partner and fertility outcomes in couples with mild male factor infertility.

item.title: Male Infertility is associated with poor Vitamin D Receptor
Poor male fertility is association with poor Vitamin D Receptor (less D to the cells)
Low Vitamin D decreases fertility and increases subsequent autism, ADHD, etc
Vitamin D and Male Fertility Review
Unexplained infertility 4X more likely if poor vitamin D receptor
In Vitro Fertilization clinical trial using 50,000 IU of Vitamin D bi-weekly
item.description:
Effect of Vitamin D Supplementation in Live Birth Rate of in Vitro Fertilization Outcome

NCT04547530

“Subjects allocated to the vitamin D group will take vitamin D 50,000IU per week from recruitment for 4 weeks, followed by 50,000IU once every 2 weeks throughout the IVF cycle until fetal viability is confirmed at 6 weeks (if pregnant), after which they will be switched to Materna. If not pregnant, they will continue Vitamin D 50,000IU once every 2 weeks until 6 months from randomization, during which they can undergo frozen-thawed embryo transfer.”

item.title: Sperm not improved enough by Vitamin D - 50,000 IU weekly for 8 weeks - RCT
item.description:
Evaluation of the effect of vitamin D3 supplementation on quantitative and qualitative parameters of spermograms and hormones in infertile men: A randomized controlled trial

Complementary Therapies in Medicine, Volume 53, September 2020, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ctim.2020.102529

panelLeilaAminiaRobabeMohammadbeigibMohammadrezaVafacHamidHaghanidAmirVahedian-AzimieLeilaKarimifShayestehJahanfargTannazJamialahmadihijAfsanehTalebiaAmirhosseinSahebkarklmn

Dosing just got half to 35 ng - not enough

Expect higher level/better results if they had used any of the following

  • 50,000 every 5 days for 8 weeks

  • 100,000 every week for 6 weeks

  • 50,000 weekly for 12 weeks

item.title: Live birth 1.7 X more likely after IVF if good level of vitamin D – meta-analysis
If diagnosed infertile, more likely to have live birth if Vitamin D fortification
Boys who were born Small for Gestational age are 1.9X more likely to be infertile
item.description:
Birth weight for gestational age and the risk of infertility: a Danish cohort study.

Hum Reprod. 2019 Dec 13. pii: dez232. doi: 10.1093/humrep/dez232.

Thorsted A1, Lauridsen J1, Høyer B2, Arendt L2, Bech B2, Toft G3, Hougaard K4, Olsen J2, Bonde JP5, Ramlau-Hansen C2.

Infertility risk of 1.88 X, when “corrected” for mother’s
smoking (reduces Vitamin D) and
drinking (reduces Vitamin D)
became 1.55 X

  • 60 percent more life births after infertility diagnosis if Vitamin D fortification (Denmark) - Nov 2019

item.title: Conception within 6 months 3X more likely if high vitamin D
item.description:
Pre-conception 25-hydroxyvitamin D (25(OH)D) and fecundability.

Hum Reprod. 2019 Oct 28. pii: dez170. doi: 10.1093/humrep/dez170.

#### Results of this study
< 20 ng 30-40 ng >50 ng
Conception take > 6 months 51% 28% 15%

10% higher fecundability with each 10 ng/ml

Note: average age 33, mean Vitamin D level 36 ng/ml

Grassroots Health Article on the study

item.title: Fertility (sperm) associated with vitamin D – meta-analysis
Poor ovarian response (poor IVF) associated with low vitamin D
item.description:
Poor ovarian response is associated with serum vitamin D levels and pro-inflammatory immune responses in women undergoing in-vitro fertilization

Journal of Reproductive Immunology, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jri.2019.102617

LiWuacJean AlyxaVendiolabMaria DinorahSalazar GarciaaNayoungSungaAnnieSkariahaAliceGilman-SachsbSvetlanaDambaevabJoanneKwak-Kimab

  • In-vitro Fertilization costs at least 10,000 dollars, Vitamin D costs 5 dollars

  • [tiki-download_file.php?fileId=1756] PDF

item.title: In-vitro Fertilization costs at least 10,000 dollars, Vitamin D costs 5 dollars
item.description:
Considering IVF? Here’s How Much It Costs In Canada Sept 2019

“Infertility affects one in six heterosexual couples in Canada”

One round of IVF costs $10,000 to $20,000

“IVF gets expensive because the most any province will cover is one round… and on average, it takes 2.7 rounds of IVF in order to get pregnant.

How an Israeli Startup Is Using AI to Help People Make Babies Jan 2021

  • " Insurance rarely covers IVF, and the costs run anywhere from $12,000 to $25,000 per cycle (a cycle takes about a month and includes stimulating a woman’s ovaries to produce eggs, extracting the eggs, inseminating them outside the body, and implanting an embryo)."

item.title: Vitamin D associated with 60 percent better assisted reproduction success
item.description:
Vitamin D and assisted reproductive treatment outcome: a prospective cohort study

Reproductive Health, December 2019, 16:106

Justin ChuIoannis Gallos Aurelio Tobias Lynne Robinson Jackson Kirkman-Brown Rima Dhillon-Smith Hoda Harb Abey Eapen Madhurima Rajkhowa Arri Coomarasamy

[tiki-download_file.php?fileId=1756] PDF

item.title: Fertility of men and women reduced by obesity and low vitamin D (perhaps synergistically)
Infertility treatment needs to achieve at least 30 ng of vitamin D
Natural pregnancy 4X more likely if sperm had a lot of CYP24A1
Infertile women 10 percent more likely to die (perhaps due disease associated with low vitamin D
item.description:
Infertile Women At Greater Risk Of Early Death, Study Finds

  • “Interestingly, some cancers, such as breast cancer, were found to be highly linked to a woman’s infertility, while others, including ovarian and endometrial cancers, did not develop at heightened levels.”

  • “… 70 percent more likely than healthy women to die from diabetes,…”

item.title: Perhaps vitamin D reduces DNA fragmentation of sperm – 2021
item.description:
Vitamin D and Human Sperm DNA Fragmentation Clinical Trial

Start Feb 2019,

End Feb 2020

item.title: Sexual disfunction treated by 300,000 IU of Vitamin D – RCT
item.description:
• Effect of Vitamin D Therapy on Sexual Function in Women with Sexual Dysfunction and Vitamin D Deficiency: a Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Clinical Trial.
      • See also Women had better sexual desire, orgasm and satisfaction after Vitamin D supplementation – Feb 2018
      • 25 citations of this study as of April 2023

Effect of Vitamin D Therapy on Sexual Function in Women with Sexual Dysfunction and Vitamin D Deficiency: a Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Clinical Trial.

J Urol. 2018 Nov 2. pii: S0022-5347(18)44048-7. doi: 10.1016/j.juro.2018.10.019.

item.title: The role of vitamin D in male fertility, A focus on the testis
Premature ejaculation associated with low vitamin D – 2018, 2019
item.description:
There are 2 studies on this page

Serum vitamin D level may be a novel potential risk factor for premature ejaculation: a comparative study - Aug 2018

International Urology and Nephrology, pp 1–6 https://doi.org/10.1007/s11255-018-1975-x

Alaa Mohamed Abd El aalSameh Fayek GamalEl DinLaila Ahmed Rashed Abd El Rahman Bakry Tawfik Mohammed Said El Sheemy mohammedshemy@yahoo.com

36 ng premature vs 60 ng for “mature”

Conclusion: Need > 51 ng avoid premature ejaculation

Personal note by Henry Lahore, founder of VitaminDWiki - Aug 2018

I have always lived in Western Washington, which has the most clouds in the US and had previously suffered from
